records.ety: [OE. stalke, fr. AS. stael, stel, a stalk. See Stale a handle, Stall.]

defs: The stem or main axis of a plant; as, a stalk of wheat, rye, or oats; the stalks of maize or hemp.
The petiole, pedicel, or peduncle, of a plant.
That which resembles the stalk of a plant, as the stem of a quill.
An ornament in the Corinthian capital resembling the stalk of a plant, from which the volutes and helices spring.
One of the two upright pieces of a ladder.
quotes: To climb by the rungs and the stalks.

defs: A stem or peduncle, as of certain barnacles and crinoids.
The narrow basal portion of the abdomen of a hymenopterous insect.
The peduncle of the eyes of decapod crustaceans.
An iron bar with projections inserted in a core to strengthen it; a core arbor.

records.ety: [AS. staelcan, stealcian to go slowly; cf. stealc high, elevated, Dan. stalke to stalk; probably akin to 1st stalk.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stalked (stkt); p. pr. & vb. n. Stalking.]

defs: To walk slowly and cautiously; to walk in a stealthy, noiseless manner; -- sometimes used with a reflexive pronoun.
quotes: Into the chamber he stalked him full still.
[Bertran] stalks close behind her, like a witch's fiend, Pressing to be employed.

defs: To walk behind something as a screen, for the purpose of approaching game; to proceed under cover.
quotes: The king . . . crept under the shoulder of his led horse; . . . “I must stalk,” said he.
One underneath his horse, to get a shoot doth stalk.

defs: To walk with high and proud steps; -- usually implying the affectation of dignity, and indicating dislike. The word is used, however, especially by the poets, to express dignity of step.
quotes: With manly mien he stalked along the ground.
Then stalking through the deep, He fords the ocean.
I forbear myself from entering the lists in which he has long stalked alone and unchallenged.

defs: To approach under cover of a screen, or by stealth, for the purpose of killing, as game.
quotes: As for shooting a man from behind a wall, it is cruelly like to stalking a deer.

defs: To follow (a person) persistently, with or without attempts to evade detection; as, the paparazzi stalk celebrities to get candid photographs; obsessed fans may stalk their favorite movie stars.
A high, proud, stately step or walk.
quotes: Thus twice before, . . . With martial stalk hath he gone by our watch.
The which with monstrous stalk behind him stepped.

defs: The act or process of stalking.
quotes: When the stalk was over (the antelope took alarm and ran off before I was within rifle shot) I came back.
